title: Arrhenatherum umbrosum
taxonomy: Arrhenatherum umbrosum is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Poaceae, and is further grouped under the tribe Stipeae. This classification places it in the category of grasses. The plant is a member of the genus Arrhenatherum, which consists of several species of tall grasses. In some classifications, Arrhenatherum umbrosum is also referred to as Elymus umbrosus, although both names are used to refer to the same plant. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Arrhenatherum umbrosum, commonly known as sweet bent or bent grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia. The health benefits of Arrhenatherum umbrosum include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections.
